runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Ruby", "build_system": "bundler", "package_manager": "gem", "build_commands": ["gem build bundler.gemspec", "gem install bundler-*.gem"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update && apt-get install -y graphviz", "apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.2", "system_packages": ["graphviz"]}, "repo_type": "library", "base_image": "ruby:slim-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Slim Debian-based Ruby 3.2 image optimized for building Ruby gems.", "pitfalls": ["Gem dependencies are resolved at install time; network access is required during build to fetch dependencies.", "Man page generation requires ronn (or vendored assets); ensure it is installed or vendored before building.", "Tests like spec:realworld may require graphviz and other system packages; include them in install commands if you want to run tests in CI."], "notes": "This blueprint targets building Bundler from source. The base image intentionally uses slim variant to minimize image size; for full test parity with CI, you may want to install additional build tools (gcc, make) if any native extensions are introduced."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Generated by BuildAgent based on repository context.
# Builds Bundler from source using a slim Ruby base image.

FROM ruby:slim-bookworm

# Use bash as the default shell to improve quoting behavior in smoke tests
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-lc"]

# Install system dependencies required for building Bundler and optional tooling.
RUN apt-get update -y \
  && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends graphviz \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set a working directory for the build context
WORKDIR /work

# Copy source code into the image
COPY . /work

# Build Bundler from source and install the produced gem
RUN gem build bundler.gemspec \
  && gem install bundler-*.gem

# Verify the installation
RUN bundler -v

# Default command: show Bundler version when container starts
CMD ["bundle", "-v"]
